skill.name: statistical-analysis
skill.description: Guides statistical test selection, assumption checking, effect size interpretation, power analysis, Bayesian methods, and APA-style reporting for research data.
skill.summary: Plan, check, run, and report statistical analyses for research data.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences, statistical notation, and documentation links. The only real risk factor is a local Python helper script for statistical assumption checks, with no evidence of shell execution, network requests, credential access, file deletion, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown code fences and inline code used for statistical Python examples and decision trees. I found no Ruby backtick execution or shell invocation in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown fences or Python/statistical examples, not executable Ruby or shell code. The reviewed context shows educational examples rather than command execution.

low_findings.title: Statistical Notation Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings correspond to statistical terms such as alpha levels, p-values, confidence intervals, and Bayesian probability notation. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic implementation was found in the reviewed evidence.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content discusses statistical methodology and reporting conventions. There is no semantic evidence of weak cryptographic algorithms or security-sensitive cryptography.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Misclassified as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: The network findings are static URLs in reference text, including an APA style resource and an example OSF data availability statement. These Markdown links do not perform outbound requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is documentation-only Markdown text. Repository search found no requests, urllib, sockets, curl, wget, or similar network execution code.

low_findings.title: Local Statistical Helper Script Requires Normal Script Review
low_findings.description: The skill includes a Python helper module for assumption checks using NumPy, pandas, SciPy, matplotlib, and seaborn. It processes local data and plots diagnostics; no evidence found for shell execution, network access, secret access, or destructive file operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script is real executable code, so it is a valid risk factor. Confidence is high that it is low risk because inspected imports and functions are local statistical calculations and plotting.

content.user_title: Analyze Research Data with Statistical Tests
content.value_statement: Choosing the wrong statistical test can weaken research conclusions. This skill guides test selection, assumption checks, effect sizes, power analysis, and APA reporting.
actual_capabilities: Guides selection of t-tests, ANOVA, chi-square tests, correlation, regression, and non-parametric alternatives.
Checks statistical assumptions with an included Python helper for normality, variance, linearity, and outliers.
Explains effect sizes, confidence intervals, and practical significance for common research designs.
Supports power analysis and sample size planning with statsmodels and pingouin examples.
Provides Bayesian analysis guidance, including priors, credible intervals, Bayes factors, and diagnostics.
Drafts APA-style statistical results with assumptions, test statistics, effect sizes, and limitations.
limitations: It is guidance for analysis and reporting, not a substitute for statistical peer review.
It requires users to provide clean data, variable definitions, and study design details.
The Python helper depends on common scientific Python libraries installed by the user.
It does not automatically validate every causal claim or research design assumption.

use_cases.title: Plan a Research Analysis
use_cases.description: Choose tests, define assumptions, plan power analysis, and prepare a transparent analysis workflow before collecting data.

use_cases.title: Review Statistical Results
use_cases.description: Check whether reported tests, assumptions, effect sizes, and interpretations match the study design and data type.

use_cases.title: Prepare Publication Reporting
use_cases.description: Convert analysis outputs into clear APA-style results with statistics, confidence intervals, and practical interpretation.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Test
prompt_templates.prompt: I have [outcome type], [number of groups], and [independent or paired observations]. Help me choose the right statistical test and explain the assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Check Assumptions
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my study design and variables. Tell me which assumptions to check, which diagnostics to run, and what to report if assumptions fail.

prompt_templates.title: Interpret Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Here are my descriptive statistics and test output: [paste results]. Write an interpretation with effect size, confidence interval, and practical significance.

prompt_templates.title: Build Analysis Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a full statistical analysis plan for this research question: [question]. Include test selection, power analysis, assumptions, corrections, sensitivity checks, and APA reporting.

output_examples.input: Two independent groups with a continuous score and unequal variances.
output_examples.output: Use Welch's independent samples t-test. Report group means, standard deviations, test statistic, degrees of freedom, p-value, Cohen's d, confidence interval, and variance assumption handling.

output_examples.input: A regression model with three predictors and concerns about multicollinearity.
output_examples.output: Check residual plots, normality of residuals, heteroscedasticity, influential points, and variance inflation factors. Report adjusted R squared, coefficients, intervals, and diagnostic decisions.

output_examples.input: A small study where prior evidence is available.
output_examples.output: Consider a Bayesian model with justified priors. Report posterior estimates, credible intervals, convergence diagnostics, and how prior assumptions affect conclusions.

best_practices: Define the research question, outcome type, group structure, and planned comparisons before choosing tests.
Check assumptions before interpreting results, and report any corrective actions clearly.
Report effect sizes and intervals alongside p-values to separate statistical and practical significance.
anti_patterns: Do not try many tests until one result becomes significant.
Do not report p-values without effect sizes, assumptions, and sample sizes.
Do not treat non-significant results as proof that no effect exists.

faq.question: Can this skill run statistical tests for me?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and examples. It can help prepare code, but users must provide data and verify outputs.

faq.question: Does it support Bayesian analysis?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for priors, Bayesian tests, credible intervals, Bayes factors, and convergence checks.

faq.question: Can it help with APA reporting?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes templates and examples for test statistics, p-values, effect sizes, intervals, and assumptions.

faq.question: What data formats does it require?
faq.answer: The guidance is format-neutral. The helper script expects Python arrays, pandas Series, or pandas DataFrames.

faq.question: Is it suitable for clinical or regulated decisions?
faq.answer: Use it as analytical support only. Regulated or high-stakes decisions need qualified statistical and domain review.

faq.question: What tools work with this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
